books.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
books.author: Martin Kleppmann
books.blurb: The reference for how reliable, scalable systems actually store and move data — replication, partitioning, and the tradeoffs behind every database pitch deck.

books.title: Deep Learning
books.author: Goodfellow, Bengio & Courville
books.blurb: The textbook. Dense, but it's the reason 'backprop through a transformer' stops being a black box.

books.title: Advances in Financial Machine Learning
books.author: Marcos López de Prado
books.blurb: Why most backtests lie, and a rebuild of the ML pipeline for finance from labeling to cross-validation.

books.title: Building Applications with Large Language Models
books.author: Valentina Alto
books.blurb: Practical patterns for wiring LLMs into real products — retrieval, tool use, and the glue code nobody puts in the paper.

books.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Practice
books.author: Keith Bourne
books.blurb: Came up twice this month scouting the RAG topic on the tech radar — queued to see if it's worth the reading time.

books.title: Prompt Engineering for Generative AI
books.author: James Phoenix & Mike Taylor
books.blurb: Solid primer, aged fast — useful for the fundamentals, less so for anything model-specific.

books.title: Algorithmic Trading
books.author: Ernest P. Chan
books.blurb: Mean reversion, momentum, and the unglamorous plumbing of actually running a strategy live.

books.title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction
books.author: Sutton & Barto
books.blurb: The canonical RL text — added after it kept getting cited in agent-training threads.

books.title: Agentic Design Patterns
books.author: Antonio Gulli
books.blurb: A pattern catalog for multi-step agent workflows — planning, reflection, tool routing.

books.title: Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives
books.author: John C. Hull
books.blurb: Still the derivatives bible. Long, but every chapter earns its place.

books.title: Team Topologies
books.author: Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais
books.blurb: Recommended by three separate engineers this quarter — finally queuing it.

books.title: Streaming Systems
books.author: Tyler Akidau, Slava Chernyak & Reuven Lax
books.blurb: Windowing, watermarks, and exactly-once semantics — relevant to the scraper pipeline's next rewrite.

books.title: Understanding Deep Learning
books.author: Simon J.D. Prince
books.blurb: A newer, more visual take on the deep learning fundamentals — queued as a companion to the Goodfellow text.
